items.why_it_matters: A user who names the actual concern is much easier to route into a useful next step than someone who only says they want to learn something.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the concern and keep gathering timing plus first-visit facts.

items.why_it_matters: Timing is one of the clearest signs that a trial inquiry is moving toward action.
items.echoer_action: Keep the reply warm and direct the next step toward booking facts.

trigger_patterns: not sure if it suits me
items.why_it_matters: First-time caution means the reply should optimize for reassurance and trust, not for closing speed.
items.echoer_action: Use calm language and guide toward a trial-style next step.

items.why_it_matters: Deadline-driven trial requests often convert quickly if slot and concern fit are handled well.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge urgency and ask for deadline plus available slot.

items.why_it_matters: Price-only questions are not low intent by default, but they need concern scope before the next step becomes useful.
items.echoer_action: Answer lightly and recover one scope detail.

items.why_it_matters: When the concern is personal and exposed in public, the response must protect dignity while staying useful.
items.echoer_action: Reply gently, avoid echoing the shame language, and guide toward a safer next step.
